# Maestro — Claude Code Plugin

> Full-stack autonomous development orchestrator. Build features with `/maestro`. Build products with `/maestro magnum-opus`.

## Overview

Maestro is a Claude Code plugin that orchestrates autonomous software development across three layers: Vision & Strategy, Tactics & Architecture, and Execution. It decomposes features into stories, dispatches specialized agents, runs QA, and ships clean commits — while tracking cost and learning from every session.

## Commands

- /maestro "task" — Build a feature autonomously (decompose → implement → QA → commit)
- /maestro magnum-opus "vision" — Build entire products (interview → research → roadmap → milestones)
- /maestro init — Initialize for a project (auto-discover tech stack)
- /maestro status — View progress, resume, pause, or abort
- /maestro plan "task" — Deep planning with codebase exploration before execution
- /maestro board — Kanban board view with external sync
- /maestro brain — Second brain (Obsidian/Notion knowledge base)
- /maestro doctor — Health diagnostics
- /maestro config — View/edit configuration
- /maestro model — View/change model assignments per task type
- /maestro help [topic] — Contextual help and FAQ
- /maestro viz — Mermaid diagrams and ASCII dashboards
- /maestro deps — Story dependency graph
- /maestro spec — Structured feature specifications
- /maestro notify — Push notifications (Slack, Discord, Telegram)
- /maestro history — Past sessions and cost analysis
- /maestro cost-estimate — Token cost forecast before building
- /maestro rollback — Revert changes with git + kanban sync
- /maestro demo — Interactive walkthrough
- /maestro quick-start — Pre-built task templates
- /maestro retro — Weekly retrospective with shipping stats and improvements
- /maestro profile — Switch configuration profiles

## Execution Modes

- --yolo: Auto-approve everything, maximum speed
- --checkpoint: Pause after each story for review (default)
- --careful: Pause after each phase for granular control

## 7-Phase Dev Loop

VALIDATE → DELEGATE → IMPLEMENT → SELF-HEAL → QA → GIT CRAFT → CHECKPOINT

## Agents

- Implementer (Sonnet): TDD story implementation in isolated worktrees
- QA Reviewer (Opus): Read-only skeptical code review with confidence scoring
- Researcher (Sonnet): Web search + Playwright competitive analysis
- Strategist (Opus): Marketing, growth, positioning
- Fixer (Sonnet): Targeted error fixes during self-heal
- Proactive (Haiku): Background monitoring, health checks

## Key Skills (75 total)

Core: dev-loop, opus-loop, decompose, delegation, classifier, context-engine, model-router
Quality: multi-review, gcr-loop, test-gen, commit-score, git-craft, qa-reviewer, semantic-diff
Intelligence: memory, retrospective, self-correct, feature-registry, steering, audit-log
Safety: doom-loop, checkpoint, security-drift, index-health, speculative, rules-doctor
Cost: cost-dashboard, token-ledger, forecast, benchmark
Integration: kanban, brain, notify, webhooks, scheduler, watch, ci-watch, audio, remote-control

## Integrations

- Project Management: GitHub Issues, Asana, Jira, Linear
- Knowledge Base: Obsidian, Notion
- Notifications: Slack, Discord, Telegram
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI

## Progressive Trust

Novice → Apprentice → Journeyman → Expert (based on QA first-pass rate across stories)

## File Structure

.maestro/dna.md — Project DNA (tech stack, patterns, conventions)
.maestro/config.yaml — Configuration
.maestro/trust.yaml — Trust metrics
.maestro/state.local.md — Active session state
.maestro/stories/ — Story spec files
.maestro/memory/ — Semantic + episodic memory
